decisions.rationale: Greg said 'fix what needs to be fixed in M009 once and for all' — these three are not user-visible M009 quality issues. Item 5 is forensic dead-end; items 6/7 are test infrastructure that doesn't affect daily prod behavior. Defer to v2.5.1 substrate cleanup or fold into M010.

decisions.decision: Sequence #9 first because of TONIGHT 2026-05-10 20:00 Paris weekly_review fire deadline
decisions.rationale: First-ever weekly_review fire in production; current prompt produces templated fallback on adversarial input per TEST-31 2026-05-07 failure. Fix + deploy before 20:00 Paris means tonight's fire produces a real Sonnet observation, not the static fallback string.
Greg is tired of waiting and tired of process overhead. Each item is small enough for direct execution + atomic commit.

decisions.decision: Two deploy points: after #9 (so tonight's fire benefits), after all 6 (final)
decisions.rationale: Single deploy after all 6 would miss tonight's deadline. Six per-item deploys would be excessive. Two strikes the balance.
